---
title: "Matilda tutorial 5 — Data simulation"
output: html_document
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

Generate synthetic multimodal cells from the trained VAE decoders — useful for
augmenting rare populations or building balanced references.

```{r setup}
source("_setup.R")
ref <- if (file.exists("matilda_teaseq_ref.rds")) readRDS("matilda_teaseq_ref.rds") else
  matilda_train(load_teaseq_sce("train"), label = "cell_type")
```

## Simulate

```{r simulate}
sim <- matilda_simulate(ref, celltype = "B.Naive", n = 200)
sim                       # a SingleCellExperiment of simulated cells
table(sim$label)
```

Use `celltype = NULL` (or `-1`) to simulate every cell type at once.

## Performance validation

Simulated values are **bit-identical** to the original Matilda Python
implementation (max|Δ| = 0). See `inst/scripts/parity_check.R`.
